input.instruction: A weakener fired on a credibility evidence package. Using ONLY the supplied pattern definition and measures, explain what the flag means, why it fired, and what the engineer should do. Choose ONE action_class from the §5B vocabulary, report a verbalized confidence, and escalate if uncertain. Stay within measure-don't-judge: report on the evidence package, never on whether the model is correct.

fired_pattern.definition: Prediction-interval coverage is below nominal; COU-relevance of the gap is unclear.

case_context.cou: the prediction interval is used directly as a risk bound
case_context.decision_consequence: risk bound taken from the interval

answer_key.gold_mechanism: Empirical coverage 0.82 vs nominal 0.95 means the intervals are over-confident; since the COU uses the interval as a risk bound, the under-coverage propagates into an under-stated risk the decision relies on.

gold_action.posture_basis: standard-derived (ASME V&V 40: credibility bounded to the validated domain)

parameters.scope: recalibrate / widen the intervals to nominal coverage
parameters.blocks: using the interval as a risk bound as-is

forbidden_claims: asserts the predictions are wrong (a verdict the miscalibration does not establish)
treats 0.82 coverage as adequate for a 0.95-nominal risk bound

hardness.obvious_signal: prediction intervals are reported and roughly the right size (mean width plausible)
hardness.conflicting_signal: the empirical coverage is 0.82 against a nominal 0.95 — the intervals are systematically too narrow (over-confident), and the COU consumes the interval as a risk bound, so the under-coverage propagates into an under-stated risk

label_provenance.external_grounding: ASME V&V 40 uncertainty: under-covering intervals used as a risk bound understate risk; recalibration is needed before reliance.

by_field.gold_action.selected_class: provisional-self-adjudicated (row-builder; needs independent expert/solver adjudication before disposition-gate use)
